DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JASON TRAVIS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES IS DENIED. AFTER THE FILING OF THIS MOTION, DEFENDANT PROVIDED VERIFIED FURTHER ANSWERS TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AT ISSUE. IF PLAINTIFF BELIEVES THERE ARE ANY DEFICIENCIES IN THE FURTHER ANSWERS, HE SHOULD MEET-AND-CONFER ABOUT THEM AND, IF NECESSARY, FILE A NEW MOTION RELATED TO THE OPERATIVE ANSWERS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. BASED ON THE APRIL 30, 2019, LETTER (KAHN DECL. EX. A), DEFENSE COUNSEL OFFERED AN EXTENSION TO MEET AND CONFER ABOUT THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AT ISSUE, INCLUDING ABOUT THE APRIL 9, 2019, LETTER FOR WHICH DELIVERY IS CONTESTED. THIS WAS A REASONABLE OFFER AND COULD HAVE AVOIDED JUDICIAL INTERVENTION. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. AS PLAINTIFF POINTS OUT, THE OPPOSITION WAS FILED LATE BASED ON THE ORIGINAL HEARING DATE. WHILE I EXERCISE DISCRETION TO CONSIDER THE OPPOSITION AND SUPPORTING MATERIALS, THE LATE FILING IS GROUNDS TO DENY THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLAINCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SBORROWMAN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT HARRIETT WILSON, THE HARRIETT M. WILSON TRUST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING DEFENDANT'S OBLIGATION TO PROVIDE HABITABLE PREMISES/QUIET ENJOYMENT AND DEFENDANT'S KNOWLEDGE OF THE CONDITIONS OF THE PROPERTY IMMEDIATELY PRIOR TO AND DURING PLAINTIFF'S TENANCY. THESE FACTS ARE MATERIAL TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE AN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJURATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS; DUPLICATIVE OF NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT STOIBER "ENDORSED THE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS" IS INAPPOSITE. STOIBER V. HONEYCHUCK (1980) 101 CAL.APP.3D 903. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS OTHERWISE DENIED FOR THE REASONS STATED ABOVE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jason Travis, 415.421.4400 for Plaintiff Jason Travis.
Robert Kahn, 213.427.2350 for Defendant Harriett Wilson.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT HARRIETT WILSON, THE HARRIETT M. WILSON TRUST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Triable issues of fact regarding Defendant's obligation to provide habitable premises/quiet enjoyment and Defendant's knowledge of the conditions of the property immediately prior to and during Plaintiff's tenancy. These facts are material to all causes of action asserted by Plaintiff. Plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Motion for Summary Adjuration is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to the cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress; duplicative of negligence cause of action. Plaintiff's argument that Stoiber "endorsed the plaintiff's claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress" is inapposite. Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 101 Cal.App.3d 903. Motion for Summary Adjudication is otherwise denied for the reasons stated above. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Plaintiff Jason Travis' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Jason Travis' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions.

The motion to compel further responses is DENIED. After the filing of this motion, Defendant provided verified further answers to the special interrogatories at issue. If Plaintiff believes there are any deficiencies in the further answers, he should meet-and-confer about them and, if necessary, file a new motion related to the operative answers. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Based on the April 30, 2019, letter (Kahn Decl. Ex. A), defense counsel offered an extension to meet and confer about the special interrogatories at issue, including about the April 9, 2019, letter for which delivery is contested. This was a reasonable offer and could have avoided judicial intervention. Defendant's request for sanctions is DENIED. As Plaintiff points out, the opposition was filed late based on the original hearing date. While I exercise discretion to consider the opposition and supporting materials, the late filing is grounds to deny the request for sanctions.

Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complaince with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to the Judge Pro Tem at
sborrowman@gmail.com.

Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Steven S. Miyake, Esq., Telephone (415) 421-4400, appearing for Plaintiff Jason Travis;
Robert S. Kahn, Esq., Koletsky, Mancini, Feldman & Morrow, Telephone (213) 427-2350, appearing for Defendant Harriett M. Wilson; (Via Telephone)

Matter was previously set for hearing on December 20, 2018 and continued to January 3, 2019. A stipulation and order for the motion to be heard by a Judge Pro Tem was previously signed by all parties at the December 20, 2018 hearing.

ARGUED: The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS HARRIETT WILSON, THE HARRIETT M. WILSON TRUST'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Request For Order Awarding Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney In The Sum Of $1,320.00. (Specially set for 2:00 p.m. by Judge Pro Tem Bruce Highman)

Denied as to interrogatories 97, 103, 104, 112, 117, 122, 132, 133, 138, 141, 142 and 150. The information requested by these interrogatories was sufficiently requested by other interrogatories. The motion is otherwise granted. The interrogatories as to which the motion is granted are not sufficiently the same as the prior discovery requests plaintiff cites. Among other things, the court notes that: (1) the plaintiff answers deposition questions off the top of his head but has to do appropriate investigation when answering an interrogatory; and (2) one can't necessarily tell from responses to document requests which documents are being produced in response to which document request, so interrogatories asking for documents to be identified are appropriate. Request for sanctions are denied. Plaintiff has fifty days (50) from the date of entry of the order to comply. Judge Pro Tem Bruce Highman will retain jurisdiction. Counsel for defendant is to prepare and submit form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 by email to Judge Pro Tem Bruce Highman at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman; Clerk: William Trupek; Not Reported.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege extreme and outrageous conduct. Allegations in paragraphs 13, 14 and 15 are not sufficient. Plaintiff alleges that Defendant was notified orally starting April 2, 2016 (via a series of 2-4 minute connections, including two in the middle of the night at 12:18 a.m. and 12:22 a.m.) of 10 defective conditions (a)-(j); on August 18, 2018 water intrusion was cited by the DBI; on August 31, 2018 written notice of the conditions was made by Plaintiff. Having not been satisfied with Defendant's response to the oral notifications, DBI citation, and written notification, Plaintiffs vacated "in August 2016." These allegations are not sufficient to show outrageous conduct. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles Haines; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Not reported. (501/CFH)
